title: Borough, London: William & Bryony, Southwark Cathedral

main_route.original_wording: Borough in London, William's & Bryony's house
main_route.excerpt: Simon & Eve set off for Borough in London, William's & Bryony's house. We camped in the courtyard.
Simon & Eve went to Southwark Cathedral, where Canon Jeffrey John celebrated Communion for the last time before moving to be Dean of St. Albans.
We bought lunch at M & S for everyone & then went home.

summary: Simon and Eve drove from Birmingham to stay overnight at son William and his partner Bryony's house in Borough, London, camping in their courtyard. The next morning they attended a special Communion at Southwark Cathedral celebrated by Canon Jeffrey John on his last occasion before becoming Dean of St Albans, then bought lunch at M&S and returned home.
extraction_notes: Single-page entry covering a two-day trip (Sat 22 May into Sunday 23 May 2004). Only Simon and Eve are named as travellers; William, Bryony, and Bryony's mother Tess are mentioned as hosts/guests but did not travel from Birmingham. The date 'Sat 22nd May' confirms 2004 (22 May 2004 was a Saturday). St Albans is mentioned only as Jeffrey John's destination, not a stop visited. The M&S lunch stop and the ballet/restaurant ('Esh') on the Saturday evening are mentioned but are not meaningful named route stops. No ferry or international crossing — entirely within Great Britain.
